description: Fix session permission pruning that incorrectly drops browser tool 'Allow Always' rules on every reload. The pruneStaleState function in reload.ts filters sessionPermissions against registry.toolLookup, which only contains plugin tools — browser tool names are never in that map, causing all browser tool session permission rules to be silently dropped on every hot reload or config reload.

userStories.title: Preserve browser tool session permissions across reloads
userStories.description: As a user who clicked 'Allow Always' for a browser tool, I want my permission to persist across hot reloads and config reloads so that I am not re-prompted for the same tool.
acceptanceCriteria: pruneStaleState in reload.ts preserves session permission rules where rule.tool is a valid browser tool name (e.g., browser_execute_script, browser_click_element)
pruneStaleState still prunes session permission rules for plugin tools that no longer exist in the registry
pruneStaleState still preserves rules where rule.tool is null (domain_all scope)
Existing test 'prunes stale session permission rules for tools no longer in the registry' still passes
New test verifies that a session permission rule for a browser tool (e.g., browser_execute_script) survives a reload
